source: Learning Ansible with Rocky - Ansible Intermediate: Variables, external files, and register

questions.q: Which primitive variable types does the guide list?
questions.why: Primitives are strings, integers and booleans; they can be organised as lists or dictionaries.

questions.q: How do you reference a simple variable named port_http in a playbook?
questions.why: Variables are called between double braces.

questions.q: Which two syntaxes both read the 'rhel' key of a dictionary variable named service?
questions.why: Both bracket notation and dot notation address a dictionary key.

questions.q: How do you pass a variable to ansible-playbook on the command line?
questions.why: --extra-vars (or -e) supplies variables from the command line.

questions.q: Which play directive loads variables from an external file?
questions.why: vars_files lists external YAML variable files to load.

questions.q: Which module includes variables dynamically during a play?
questions.why: include_vars loads variables at runtime, e.g. file: vault.yml.

questions.q: Which module prints a variable or a message for debugging?
questions.why: The debug module shows a var: or a msg:.

questions.q: What does the `register` keyword do in a task?
options: Saves the task's return so it can be used by later tasks
Registers the host in the inventory
questions.why: register stores the result (e.g. homes.stdout, homes.stdout_lines) for later use.

questions.q: For a task registered as 'homes', what is homes.stdout_lines?
options: A list, one entry per line of output
The whole output as a single string
questions.why: stdout_lines is a list of the output lines; stdout is the whole string.
